The TUM Global Office receives a limited number of dormitory offerings (Service Package) from Munich Student Union. Students wishing to be considered for this Service Package must check the relevant box in the application form and have to be enrolled until a set deadline (to be announced after admission).

Please be aware that the private market is tense and competition for housing in Munich is high.

Website of incoming exchange student information
Courses for exchange students（部分系所限制請務必詳閱Course Restrictions分頁）
Human Medicine is restricted for exchange students coming to Tübingen through a faculty-wide agreement and students need to be fluent in German.
Courses at the Faculty of Mathematics and Natural Sciences (including Psychology) and the Faculty of Medicine: Courses must be approved in advance in the application process for your studies; proof of eligibility for a course is, for example, your transcript.
Biology on M.Sc. level is only available for students whose majour at their home university is biology or a closely related study field.
Pharmacy (all levels): only available for students whose major at their home university is pharmacy.
該校法律學院皆為德文授課，僅具英語能力者可申請至該校社會科學院，並選擇英語授課之法律領域課程
Two lectures and two seminars per semester are recommended. In addition, students can attend courses in the area of German as a foreign language. We recommend a workload which does not exceed 30 ECTS / semester.

語言能力限制.label: 語言能力限制
語言能力限制.text:
Law (all study levels), Ethnology (B.A.), Biochemistry / Chemistry (B.Sc.): Students from all partner universities must provide proof of German language proficiency of completed B1 or on a B2 level.
Biology / Molecular Medicine / Pharmacy (B.Sc.): Students from overseas partner universities must provide proof of German language proficiency of completed B1 or on a B2 level.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Application and Preparation for International Exchange Students: Here you will find important information on application, accommodation, entry regulations and enrollment.
Language courses for incoming exchange students
Information for exchange students
Exchange students will be required to pay a non-waivable student services fee (currently around € 120 per semester) to the Studierendenwerk (governmental social service organization connected to German universities). The fee is mandatory for all students (also the German students) and subsidizes the semester bus ticket, meals at the cafeteria, as well as free psychological counselling.

links.text: Application and Preparation for International Exchange Students

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
The University of Tübingen has a yearly quota of rooms that are reserved solely for exchange students. Basic info: Accomodation. Please see for details of the dormitories the Overview of residence halls by the Studierendenwerk.

All rooms for exchange students are fully furnished single rooms with shared kitchen and usually shared bathrooms. We strongly recommend students to opt for student housing because of the difficult and expensive private housing market in Tübingen.

In general, the language of instruction is German. Most study programs offered at Ulm University are German-taught programs. However, there may be able to select courses taught in English even in German study programs. In addition, there are currently 12 Master programs taught completely in English.
The students have to choose the courses which really belong to the study program. Otherwise, they will be rejected.

Website for incoming exchange student information
Courses and semester calendar
NOT open to exchange students: Medicine
It is recommended that students take between 12 and 18 contact hours per semester for a total of around 30 ECTS credits per semester.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Cost of Living
Language courses for exchange students
Insurance for students: 
Private insurance: Students can take out an insurance policy abroad that covers the European area. Please contact one of the statutory German health insurance companies, quoting sender number H0001178, to check if your insurance cover is enough to exempt you from statutory German health insurance.
Statutory insurance: If they have not arranged any private insurance or their cover is not sufficient, students can take out statutory German health insurance, which costs around €135-€150 per month. Please contact one of the statutory German health insurance companies, quoting sender number H0001178, to get insured and request an insurance number.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Students can apply for a room in a dorm owned by the Studierendenwerk Bonn via the online application page. The rental contract will be emailed to the student and will need to be signed around 6 weeks before they arrive.

More information on living in Bonn and private housing is available on the Living in Bonn webpage.

TOEFL iBT 79 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級 或 培力英檢B2(中高級)，僅具備英語能力者請詳閱下方注意事項。

Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
Courses offered completely in English
Courses from the Hector School and School of Optics and Photonics are not available for international students.
The list of courses taught in English at the Department of Economics and Management
The Faculty of Mechanical Engineering offers a bachelor degree in English and a wide range of other courses taught in English.
The following departments at KIT allow students to study completely in English if they only choose Englisch taught courses:
Department of Architecture (ARCH),
Department of Civil Engineering, Geo and Environmental Sciences (BGU),
Department of Chemical and Process Engineering (CIW),
Department of Informatics
Department of Electrical Engineering and Information Technology (ETIT),
Department of Mechanical Engineering (MACH) and
Department of Economics and Management (WIWI)

特殊學系語檢要求.label: 特殊學系語檢要求
特殊學系語檢要求.text:
The following departments at KIT:
• Department of Chemistry and Biosciences (CHEM-BIO)
require applicants to have:
• a C1 CEFR level of German language certificate if courses taught in German will be attended or
• B2/C1 CEFR level of English language certificate if courses taught in English will be attended.

The following departments at KIT:
• Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (GEIST-SOZ),
• Department of Mathematics (MATH) and
• Department of Physics (PHY)
require applicants to have:
• an A2/B1 CEFR level of German language certificate (mandatory requirement) and additionally
• a B2 CEFR level of English language certificate only if courses taught in English will be attended.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
For a good start to the semester at KIT, all incoming students are allowed to participate in a three-week German language course (beginners and intermediate A2-B2) before the semester begins. This course is not a substitute for the certificate of the German language level required for the application. The course fee is 75 euros for international students. Students will get 4 ECTS if they attend 80% of the course and pass the exam. During the semester, the Studienkolleg at KIT offers free German language courses for all students enrolled at KIT.
Health insurance coverage is mandatory for your entire stay in Germany, with unlimited coverage. You can either obtain private health insurance in your home country before arriving in Karlsruhe or take out a German public health insurance before or after arrival. However, we strongly recommend choosing German public health insurance, as many private policies from non-EU countries may not be accepted. Without valid insurance, enrolment at the university is not possible.
Students from partner universities pay a charge for the Studierendenwerk of about 109.50 € per semester. This includes reduced student prices at the university restaurant, transportation reductions, and accident insurance.
Cost of Living

links.text: pay a charge for the Studierendenwerk

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
KIT reserves rooms in student residences for students from partner universities. Accommodation is guaranteed if students adhere to the application procedure and deadlines. Details are sent by email after admission to KIT.

The furnished single rooms cost between €250 and €350 per month and are rented for fixed lease periods: October to March (winter semester) and/or April to September (summer semester). Please note that these rental periods are non-negotiable, and no alternative move-in or move-out dates are available.

Please note that accommodation prices on the private market are significantly higher than those in student dorms, and there is also a risk of scams. For this reason, we highly recommend applying for a room in a student dorm through us.

For students attending the pre-semester German language course (starting mid-September in the winter semester/mid-March in the summer semester), we recommend reserving accommodation in youth hostels, hostels, or Airbnb for the two weeks before the rental period of guaranteed rooms in the student residences. This must be arranged privately, as KIT unfortunately cannot assist with finding housing on the private market.

住宿申請注意事項.label: 住宿申請注意事項
住宿申請注意事項.text: 交換期間住宿由KIT Housing Office全權主責，且將根據全球各姊妹校的申請狀況做分配，恕無法保證安排到自己的第一順位房源。為避免造成姊妹校行政流程負擔並顧及其他同學權益，在租約（rental agreement）開立後即無法取消／放棄住宿資格，請特別留意。

Other Highlight.label: Other Highlight
Other Highlight.text:
Incomer Podcast: Ticket to Karlsruhe
The KIT, the Research University in the Helmholtz Association

sections_ordered.text: 交換期間住宿由KIT Housing Office全權主責，且將根據全球各姊妹校的申請狀況做分配，恕無法保證安排到自己的第一順位房源。為避免造成姊妹校行政流程負擔並顧及其他同學權益，在租約（rental agreement）開立後即無法取消／放棄住宿資格，請特別留意。

Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
Courses offered completely in English
Courses from the Hector School and School of Optics and Photonics are not available for international students.
The list of courses taught in English at the Department of Economics and Management
The Faculty of Mechanical Engineering offers a bachelor degree in English and a wide range of other courses taught in English.
The following departments at KIT allow students to study completely in English if they only choose Englisch taught courses:
Department of Architecture (ARCH),
Department of Civil Engineering, Geo and Environmental Sciences (BGU),
Department of Chemical and Process Engineering (CIW),
Department of Informatics
Department of Electrical Engineering and Information Technology (ETIT),
Department of Mechanical Engineering (MACH) and
Department of Economics and Management (WIWI)
housing_text:
KIT reserves rooms in student residences for students from partner universities. Accommodation is guaranteed if students adhere to the application procedure and deadlines. Details are sent by email after admission to KIT.

The furnished single rooms cost between €250 and €350 per month and are rented for fixed lease periods: October to March (winter semester) and/or April to September (summer semester). Please note that these rental periods are non-negotiable, and no alternative move-in or move-out dates are available.

Please note that accommodation prices on the private market are significantly higher than those in student dorms, and there is also a risk of scams. For this reason, we highly recommend applying for a room in a student dorm through us.

For students attending the pre-semester German language course (starting mid-September in the winter semester/mid-March in the summer semester), we recommend reserving accommodation in youth hostels, hostels, or Airbnb for the two weeks before the rental period of guaranteed rooms in the student residences. This must be arranged privately, as KIT unfortunately cannot assist with finding housing on the private market.

德語組
本校大一以上/碩一以上(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 87 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級，僅具備英語能力者，請詳閱注意事項。
或
具德語檢定成績 B2(中高級) 級以上。
不接受醫學院、公共衛生學院、法律學院、理學院心理學系、理學院心理學研究所之學生申請。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期4名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 4 人)Economics

學校年曆.text: Academic Calendar

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
該校英語授課課程有限，僅持英語檢定同學，務必留意是否有足夠英語授課之課程可選讀。
Website of incoming exchange student information.
The main language of instruction at FAU is German; in some fields English. The availability of English taught courses varies at every faculty, please check their websites for further information.
NO access to human medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, law and psychology. Also admission to certain lectures in practical sports is restricted.
Certain programmes on Bachelors level at our Faculty of Sciences are in German.
Course catalogue:     
Click on Vorlesungsverzeichnis in the menu Lehre.
Courses are listed by faculty and sorted by study programme.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
A preparatory German course will be held before the start of lectures. The course fee and its credits can be found online. During the semester period FAU language centre offers language courses in different languages on different levels.
German health insurance is obligatory. Sufficient insurance cover is required for the enrolment at FAU and in order to receive the residence permit. Accident insurance and liability insurance is highly recommended. All insurances should cover the whole stay in Germany.
Travel health insurance: non-EU citizens should have travel health insurance at least valid until the start of lectures. Please be aware that international travel insurances are not sufficient and will not be approved by FAU and German authorities.
Cost of Living: On our website, we provide a list of costs of living that are to be expected.
Information on entering Germany can be found on our websites.

Welcome Week.label: Welcome Week
Welcome Week.text:
Welcome week (obligatory): In order to help the students organise their studies and to deal with formalities, FAU offers welcome weeks for international students twice each semester, before the semester starts.
FAU is offering welcome packages for international students. Prices and offers can be found online.
International students can register for our buddy programme.

links.text: Prices and offers can be found online

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text: Exchange students will receive information on accommodation after their application. Information to housing search can be found online.

Website for incoming exchange student information
Students may take reference from previous course offerings by browsing the course catalogue.
Courses at the School of Medicine and Dentistry are NOT available for exchange students. Those by the Faculty of Engineering Sciences are only partially available.
Heidelberg University does NOT offer subjects in business, agriculture, and music (performing arts).
Restrictions may apply for some courses in biosciences or biotechnology, and for some law courses. Exceptions can be made with permission from the coordinator in Heidelberg.

Language of Instruction.label: Language of Instruction
Language of Instruction.text:
Typically, the language of instruction is German. However, there are several fields of study with a (large) variety of courses taught in English: i.e. English studies, American studies, economics, South Asian studies, and transcultural studies. A few courses in sociology, political science and psychology are also offered in English.
Some masters programs are (fully or partly) taught in English. This may also apply to advanced courses at the bachelor's level.
Information on all Master Courses
Master fully taught in English

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Exchange students whose major is not German/Germanistik are strongly encouraged to register for the 4-week intensive preparatory German language course at the International Study Center (ISZ). The course is offered before the start of the semester, in September for fall semester students and in March for spring semester students. The fee for the preparatory German language course is covered by the International Relations Office for exchange students.
Courses at the International Study Centre
Cost of Living: Approx. 900 - 1000 € / month. Please refer to the information on financial planning given on this website.
Health insurance with unlimited coverage is compulsory for all Heidelberg University students. Exchange Students are advised to take out health insurance in Germany (a highly comprehensive coverage), or to ensure to bring full and unlimited health insurance coverage from their country of residence.

links.text: Courses at the International Study Centre

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
The private housing market in Heidelberg is very tight, and living in residence halls run by the Studierendenwerk is the most affordable and most convenient way of staying in Heidelberg as an exchange student.

Types of accommodation include:
• single room sharing kitchen and bathrooms in groups of 2-4
• single room in dormitories with kitchens and bathrooms on each floor
• a small number of single apartments

Fees for residence halls: Approx. 250 - 570 € / Month; 450 € refundable deposit
More information here

該校絕大多數學士班課程為德語授課，僅具備英語能力者請務必確認是否有足夠課程可供選讀
Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
Please note that Human Medicine and Veterinary Medicine are NOT open to exchange students.
With few exceptions, all undergraduate programs are open for exchange students. Enrollment in the following courses is subject to availability of lab classes and to prior approval by the institute/department: Biochemistry, Bioinformatics and Biology. Restrictions apply in Pharmacy, Law, Business Administration and Economics.
The Law Department prefers direct exchange students from institutions who also accept law students. There are only very few places available for other exchange programs.
The School of Business Administration and Economics requires excellent German language skills (B2 level or above) for classes taught in German. There is a very limited number of classes in English, and they are not always offered regularly.
The following master's degree programs are open for postgraduate exchange students without restriction: modern German literature, philosophy, and political science. All other master's degree programs are restricted and need prior approval from the institute/department.

Special Courses for Exchange Students.label: Special Courses for Exchange Students
Special Courses for Exchange Students.text:
"Berlin and Germany in a European perspective"
Program of studies exclusively for exchange students
Cover key aspects of German history, culture and society in a European perspective
Courses taught in German and English / 5 ECTS credits
Examples:
Orientation in Berlin and Germany (GER)
Central Aspects of German and European Society and Culture (GER)
Key Issues of German and European Culture (GER)
Film, Art and Literature in a Social and Cultural Context (GER)
Aspects of German and European Culture (ENG)
German and European Politics (ENG)
German Media – Media in Germany (ENG)
German History in a European Context (ENG)
German Economy and Economics in Germany and Europe (ENG)

links.text: "Berlin and Germany in a European perspective"

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
The Accommodation Service offers a variety of housing options, including dorms, shared apartments, and fully furnished single apartments. Availability runs from the beginning of September/October or March/April onwards. Costs range from about € 500 to € 1200 per month.

Since there has been a dramatic increase in demand for student housing, students wishing to use the Accommodation Service must apply online no later than December 10 or June 10, using the link above. Registration requests are processed on a first-come, first-served basis.

*根據歷屆交換生指出，FU有三種宿舍房型，有兩種是簽六個月的合約，另一種則是可以直接簽到一年的。若選擇前兩者，第二學期將無法直接從住宿組登記到宿舍，請往後欲申請宿舍的同學特別留意。

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
一般組
本校大一以上/碩一/碩二(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
GPA 達3.00分以上。
TOEFL iBT 90 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5 或 全民英檢中高級。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期2名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 2 人)

學校年曆.label: 學校年曆
學校年曆.text: Academic Calendar

註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
Special Courses open to all the incoming students:
Greek Language Course (6 ECTS): Speak Greek, like a Greek!
Contemporary Greece: History, Arts & Letters (6 ECTS): An introductory course to Greek history and culture since the creation of the Modern Greek State.
Issues in Greek Music & Culture (6 ECTS): From Byzantine music to traditional and popular music
Recommended academic workload per semester: 25-30 ECTS

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Budget for accommodation and studies
Accommodation: A one-bedroom student flat costs approximately €300-400 and furnishing it may cost up to €1000.
Food: Students can eat free of charge at campus restaurants and there also are many affordable options in the city of Athens.
Public Transport (student discount): 0,50€ valid for 90-minutes/ 30-day fare: 13,5€ / 180-day fare for countless rides: 77,50€
VISA application information: Non-European Union citizens need a study visa to enter the country, which may take up to 3 months to be issued! Study visas are issued by the Greek consulate of the country of origin and are of type D (Visa D 1.1). Visa applications should be made upon acceptance to your programme of choice. In case of entering the country with a tourist visa or a Schengen visa for another country, you cannot change it into a student residence permit.
Private insurance contract for non-European Union students: The insurance contract to be submitted with the residency permit or its extension, should include:
Health care in public hospitals: 10.000€ annually with owncontribution of 20%.
Permanent (total or partial) impairment insurance caused by an accident: 15.000€ annually with own contribution of 20%.
Illness or accident-related medical and pharmaceutical expenses: 1.500€ annually with own contribution of 20%.
The contract should indicate that it covers expenses defined by the law/ministerial decision and should explicitly mention that it covers everything that might occur to the student during their stay in the country. If there has already been another insurance contract, from your country of origin, it can be used if officially translated into Greek, as long as it includes the above provisions. In any other case, students may sign a Greek financial contract agreement with an insurance company. The cost starts from 60€ per year.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
The University of Athens runs a Student Residence Complex (FEPA/ΦΕΠΑ in Greek) on campus consisting of 4 buildings (A, B, C, D), which can house 1064 students.

In addition to student residences, there are various ways to find a flat in Athens, ranging from advertisements to agencies and digital services.

Most apartments are unfurnished, while those near the city centre are usually old or recently renovated. Rent costs don't include public utility services (electricity, water, heating, telephone line, and internet). There is also a typical extra cost, a building fee, which is shared with the tenants, for services such as cleaners, gardeners, elevator maintenance, electricity for shared building spaces, etc.

Renting an apartment requires signing a lease contract, which is necessary for establishing tenant rights and responsibilities.

More information here.

希臘居留證注意事項.label: 希臘居留證注意事項
希臘居留證注意事項.text: 根據學長姊經驗指出，由於交換生無法申請Type-D Visa（學生簽證），可能會影響當地居留證申請資格，請有意前往當地交換的同學特別留意。（2025.11.26更新）

sections_ordered.label: 此校開放予第二次出國交換之同學選填

Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
Special Courses open to all the incoming students:
Greek Language Course (6 ECTS): Speak Greek, like a Greek!
Contemporary Greece: History, Arts & Letters (6 ECTS): An introductory course to Greek history and culture since the creation of the Modern Greek State.
Issues in Greek Music & Culture (6 ECTS): From Byzantine music to traditional and popular music
Recommended academic workload per semester: 25-30 ECTS

sections_ordered.label: 實用資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Budget for accommodation and studies
Accommodation: A one-bedroom student flat costs approximately €300-400 and furnishing it may cost up to €1000.
Food: Students can eat free of charge at campus restaurants and there also are many affordable options in the city of Athens.
Public Transport (student discount): 0,50€ valid for 90-minutes/ 30-day fare: 13,5€ / 180-day fare for countless rides: 77,50€
VISA application information: Non-European Union citizens need a study visa to enter the country, which may take up to 3 months to be issued! Study visas are issued by the Greek consulate of the country of origin and are of type D (Visa D 1.1). Visa applications should be made upon acceptance to your programme of choice. In case of entering the country with a tourist visa or a Schengen visa for another country, you cannot change it into a student residence permit.
Private insurance contract for non-European Union students: The insurance contract to be submitted with the residency permit or its extension, should include:
Health care in public hospitals: 10.000€ annually with owncontribution of 20%.
Permanent (total or partial) impairment insurance caused by an accident: 15.000€ annually with own contribution of 20%.
Illness or accident-related medical and pharmaceutical expenses: 1.500€ annually with own contribution of 20%.
The contract should indicate that it covers expenses defined by the law/ministerial decision and should explicitly mention that it covers everything that might occur to the student during their stay in the country. If there has already been another insurance contract, from your country of origin, it can be used if officially translated into Greek, as long as it includes the above provisions. In any other case, students may sign a Greek financial contract agreement with an insurance company. The cost starts from 60€ per year.

sections_ordered.label: 住宿資訊
sections_ordered.text:
The University of Athens runs a Student Residence Complex (FEPA/ΦΕΠΑ in Greek) on campus consisting of 4 buildings (A, B, C, D), which can house 1064 students.

In addition to student residences, there are various ways to find a flat in Athens, ranging from advertisements to agencies and digital services.

Most apartments are unfurnished, while those near the city centre are usually old or recently renovated. Rent costs don't include public utility services (electricity, water, heating, telephone line, and internet). There is also a typical extra cost, a building fee, which is shared with the tenants, for services such as cleaners, gardeners, elevator maintenance, electricity for shared building spaces, etc.

Renting an apartment requires signing a lease contract, which is necessary for establishing tenant rights and responsibilities.

More information here.

sections_ordered.label: 希臘居留證注意事項
sections_ordered.text: 根據學長姊經驗指出，由於交換生無法申請Type-D Visa（學生簽證），可能會影響當地居留證申請資格，請有意前往當地交換的同學特別留意。（2025.11.26更新）

Study in Hungary - Your costs of living
Students should arrange for appropriate health insurance in their home country. Those with chronic illnesses should take care to ensure that these, too, are covered by the health insurance. Students taking any medication regularly should make sure that they will have access to it during their stay in Hungary.

住宿資訊.text: While Corvinus University cannot offer on-campus (dormitory) accommodation, we do have a selected number of places that offer dormitory-like accommodation (checked by Corvinus staff members) at similar prices. Information on these will be sent out to all incoming students after the preliminary course registration period, well before their date of arrival. Information on what to keep in mind and check if arranging for accommodation individually will also be sent out to students.

Website of incoming exchange student information
The exchange students can choose only from the courses that especially announced for them in English. You can find the list of it on this website (it is updated coninuously)
The Faculty of Law can accept BA students who completed at least 4 semesters or MA students who completed at least 1 semester. In case of Biology, Chemistry and Environmental Sciences fields, the Faculty of Science at ELTE can accept only MA or PhD students.
BA students cannot take MA courses. For exchange students it is allowed to take 1 or 2 classes outside of their majors if there is capacity for it, but most of the courses should be taken from their major fields.
Minimum number of ECTS that bilateral exchange students should register (per semester) is 10 ECTS. There's no maximum limit.

Health Insurance: There is a mandatory insurance of our institution that exchange students must purchase, the cost of it can be very varying upon the chosen insurance package.

住宿資訊.text: Students can apply for dormitory places or arrange their accommodation privately. Bilateral exchange students usually got a place in our dormitory. Dormitory fee in 2-beded room: approx. HUF 70000 per month. Check this webpage.

eligibility_text:
一般組
本校大一以上/碩一以上/博一以上學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 79 (接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級。
quota_text:
一學期1名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 1 人)（因交換學生往來不平衡，此名額不開放第二次志願選填）
calendar_text: Academic Calendar
notes_text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
The exchange students can choose only from the courses that especially announced for them in English. You can find the list of it on this website (it is updated coninuously)
The Faculty of Law can accept BA students who completed at least 4 semesters or MA students who completed at least 1 semester. In case of Biology, Chemistry and Environmental Sciences fields, the Faculty of Science at ELTE can accept only MA or PhD students.
BA students cannot take MA courses. For exchange students it is allowed to take 1 or 2 classes outside of their majors if there is capacity for it, but most of the courses should be taken from their major fields.
Minimum number of ECTS that bilateral exchange students should register (per semester) is 10 ECTS. There's no maximum limit.
housing_text: Students can apply for dormitory places or arrange their accommodation privately. Bilateral exchange students usually got a place in our dormitory. Dormitory fee in 2-beded room: approx. HUF 70000 per month. Check this webpage.

TOEFL iBT 79 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5。
不接受法律學院法律學系、社會科學院經濟學系、管理學院管理學院企業管理碩士專班、理學院心理學系、理學院心理學研究所、醫學院、公共衛生學院之學生申請。

學校年曆.text: Academic Calendar

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收
Website of incoming exchange student information
Courses offered for exchange students
Courses with restrictions for exchange students:
The faculties of Medicine, Nursing, Psychology and Pharmaceutical Sciences are restricted to prior agreements between the exchanging faculties.
The faculties of Law and Economics do not accept undergraduate students
MBA is closed
Admission to the Earth Science programme is restricted to students that meet prerequisites of at least one year of geology or geophysics study.
Courses in the English Language Programme are only available to exchange students majoring in English at their home university or students who are very proficient in English (except the course "English for Practical Purposes")
Courses taught in Icelandic are generally not open to exchange students
Course selection must total 30 ECTS credits per semester, where at least 60% of courses are within the students' major

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Intensive Icelandic language courses
Student Residence Permit for non-EEA/EFTA citizens
Students from countries outside EU/EEA must have health insurance. The cost of such insurance valid for six months varies from ISK 15,000 according to the policy. Applicants for a student residence permit who must undergo a medical examination should go to the Communicable Disease Control Health Clinic (Þönglabakki 1, 109 Reykjavík) before going to the Directorate of Immigration
Living costs in Reykjavík

交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
Course details
Italian language courses are offered throughout the semester and in conjunction with the other academic coursework. The courses are recommended for all participants but not compulsory
housing_text: Students must arrange for housing on their own. More information here.

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
一般組
本校大一以上/碩一以上(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 79 (接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級。
或
具義大利語能力證明，若欲選修義大利語授課，須具備B1以上能力。
不接受醫學院、公共衛生學院之學生申請。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期2名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 2 人)

學校年曆.text:
Academic Calendar
(Calendar may vary for each School)

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
The five Campuses of the University of Bologna: Bologna, Cesena, Forlì, Ravenna and Rimini Campuses.
Information for exchange students
Course Unit Catalogue
Medicine NOT open to exchange students
Exchange students do not have to enroll in the courses before arrival. Being our university is a State university, the lectures can be attended freely. Only for a few classes (often English spoken and at Master's level), there may be a maximum number of participants. We suggest that students verify this by contacting the International Office of the School.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Italian language courses are provided free of charge by our university.
Living costs in Bologna
Medical assistance for international students
Pre-enrolment and entry VISA
Once in Italy, students must also apply for a Residency Permit.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Students must arrange for housing on their own. More information here.

If you have difficulties in finding accommodation, please get in touch with our SAIS system (support in finding accommodation for foreign students) early in the application process to ask for their support.

簽證注意事項.label: 簽證注意事項
簽證注意事項.text: ⚠️請注意，義大利學生簽證採預約制，因開放時段有限，請在收到入學許可後立即線上預約，以免無法即時取得簽證。

Website of incoming exchange student information
Courses taught in foreign languages: students must check the prerequisites of each course and make sure that you fulfill the specific admission requirements. Students should check the availability of chosen course (semester, faculty and timetable). 
Study Process and Procedures
All faculties are open if students have prerequisites. Some have limited availabilty (medicine, business).
PhD students do the research (we do not offer many courses for PhD students) so they must find supervisor for their research before coming to Lithuania.

Website of incoming exchange student information
Courses taught in foreign languages: students must check the prerequisites of each course and make sure that you fulfill the specific admission requirements. Students should check the availability of chosen course (semester, faculty and timetable). 
Study Process and Procedures
All faculties are open if students have prerequisites. Some have limited availabilty (medicine, business).
PhD students do the research (we do not offer many courses for PhD students) so they must find supervisor for their research before coming to Lithuania.

Website of incoming exchange student information
All faculties welcome exchange students, please refer to this page to see any requirements.
As full-time study is one of the visa requirements, non-EEA students need to complete at least 20 ECTS on their learning agreement at time of application. For some faculties the minimum number of ECTS may be higher, as the study load of courses varies per faculty.
有些科系可能有更高語言要求，請參考Language requirements

Cost of Living
Healthcare and Insurance
When staying in the Netherlands, students are required to have (extended): (1) Health insurance (2) Legal liability insurance for damage or injury caused to a third party (3) Travel insurance
VISA and residence permit
Social Dutch language courses are offered for free (limited spots available) for exchange students. Special discounts are also available for other language courses for students.
Orientation: Student Life and International Mobility Radboud International Students organize a Summer and Winter Intro.

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
一般組
本校大一/大二/大三學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 95 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 7.0。
不接受醫學院牙醫學系之學生申請。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期3名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 3 人)

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
NOT open to exchange students: Dentistry
Restrictions: 
Students are generally allowed to choose courses across faculties. However, for advanced courses (level 300 and up), a background in that field of study is required.
Bachelor's students are not allowed to follow courses at Master's level.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Students can take Dutch language courses at the Department of Dutch as a Second Language.
Insurance: VU Amsterdam requires all incoming international students to be properly insured by an internationally recognised insurance agency for the duration of their stay (this is also required by Dutch law). 
Living in Amsterdam can be expensive. Make sure that you have sufficient financial means to cover the costs of living. 
Experience shows that living and studying in the Netherlands for one year costs a student between €1,000 and €1,100 per month. Some students manage to spend less, but this of course depends on your own lifestyle (and type of accommodation). An indication of likely monthly expenses:
Rent for housing average: €600 (price range €366 - €1,045)
Food: €250
Insurance: €100
Public transport: €50
Books: €50
Other costs: €50
Total (average): €1,100
VISA application information

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
We strongly advise students to apply for accommodation through the International Office. Students can only start this process after their exchange application has been approved.

A handling fee of €250 per semester is required for this service. The number of rooms available is limited, accommodation cannot be guaranteed, and is assigned on a first-paid, first-served basis. Rental periods are fixed and cannot be adjusted to the student's arrival and/or departure dates.

Further information can be found on our website.

交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
Course catalogue
NOT open to exchange students: Dentistry
Restrictions: 
Students are generally allowed to choose courses across faculties. However, for advanced courses (level 300 and up), a background in that field of study is required.
Bachelor's students are not allowed to follow courses at Master's level.

sections_ordered.label: 實用資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Students can take Dutch language courses at the Department of Dutch as a Second Language.
Insurance: VU Amsterdam requires all incoming international students to be properly insured by an internationally recognised insurance agency for the duration of their stay (this is also required by Dutch law). 
Living in Amsterdam can be expensive. Make sure that you have sufficient financial means to cover the costs of living. 
Experience shows that living and studying in the Netherlands for one year costs a student between €1,000 and €1,100 per month. Some students manage to spend less, but this of course depends on your own lifestyle (and type of accommodation). An indication of likely monthly expenses:
Rent for housing average: €600 (price range €366 - €1,045)
Food: €250
Insurance: €100
Public transport: €50
Books: €50
Other costs: €50
Total (average): €1,100
VISA application information

sections_ordered.label: 住宿資訊
sections_ordered.text:
We strongly advise students to apply for accommodation through the International Office. Students can only start this process after their exchange application has been approved.

A handling fee of €250 per semester is required for this service. The number of rooms available is limited, accommodation cannot be guaranteed, and is assigned on a first-paid, first-served basis. Rental periods are fixed and cannot be adjusted to the student's arrival and/or departure dates.

Further information can be found on our website.

註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
荷蘭馬斯垂克大學與本校簽訂之交換學生計畫包括以下三個學院：It is NOT possible to take courses at different faculties during one block period (to avoid overlap in classes).
School of Business and Economics，申請該學院之交換生僅能選擇SBE提供之課程，其他學院的課程不開放，請務必詳閱交換生資訊 / 課程查詢系統。
Brochure
As an exchange student, you can only register for a maximum of two courses per period (max. 13 ECTS credits) with a minimum of one course per period (5 / 6.5 ECTS credits), and only one skills training per period (4 ECTS credits).
碩士生申請者，其學士學位亦須為經濟、商管相關領域
Faculty of Psychology and Neuroscience，申請該學院之交換生僅能選擇FPN提供之課程，該學院之荷語授課課程不開放交換生選修，請務必詳閱交換生資訊。
FPN incoming exchange guide
Course overview
There are about 10-15 study hours per week. These consist of lectures, tutorial groups, and practicals. Classes take place multiple days a week, and vary per week/period. In addition to classes, you should expect about 20 hours of independent learning per week.
University College Maastricht，申請該學院之交換生僅能選擇UCM提供之課程，請務必詳閱交換生資訊。
Minimum academic workload: 20 ECTS
Problem-Based Learning at Maastricht University

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Dutch language course
The official language at SBE (and the rest of Maastricht University) is English, but you might want to learn some Dutch. Learning Dutch will make your life as a student in Maastricht a lot easier.
If you decide to take a language course this will not be mentioned on your SBE exchange transcript, as it is not taught at SBE but the Language Centre. However, you will receive a separate Proof of Participation from the Language Centre itself.
VISA application information

生活費與保險資訊.label: 生活費與保險資訊
生活費與保險資訊.text:
Insurance
Healthcare insurance
By law, everyone in the Netherlands, even if you are here for only one or two semesters, must have healthcare insurance for medical expenses. Which type of healthcare insurance you need depends on your situation. Please check your current insurance to see if it covers your stay in the Netherlands and to what extent.
Liability insurance and household contents insurance
Make sure you are sufficiently insured for liability and household contents. More information can be found via MyMaastricht or Study in Holland.
Cost of Living

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
We must strongly advise you to arrange this urgently because available student housing in Maastricht is very limited. We strongly advise you not to come to Maastricht if you have not found housing in or around the city before the start of the academic year. Should you come to Maastricht against our advice without having arranged housing, please know that UM cannot help you because UM itself does not offer student housing, and there are no online classes.

For more information, have a look at UM Guesthouse. Please take into account the mandatory introduction days and examination days when booking a room.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Universities in the Netherlands do not own student housing. For new international Erasmus+ / Exchange students who meet the requirements, Utrecht University does offer a Reserved Accommodation Programme. This housing is reserved for new international UU students via organizations like SSH Short Stay. The housing offered through this programme is furnished and available for fixed rental periods only.

Be aware that finding housing via this programme is NOT guaranteed. The offers are limited and on first come, first served basis. You can find more information on this page.

Website of incoming exchange student information.
Courses available to non-degree students
All courses at LUC require IELTS 7.0 or TOEFL 100 (internet-based).
Programme specific language proficiency requirements for Master programmes can be found on our website.
Bachelor students are not allowed to take master level courses.
Standard academic workload: 15-30 ECTS per semester for non-degree student

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Language courses are not available as regular exchange program courses. The University's Academic Language Center offers Dutch language courses for a fee. Please note that the grades of the language courses cannot be included in the transcript.
Information for international exchange students
Travel plans

生活費與保險資訊.label: 生活費與保險資訊
生活費與保險資訊.text:
Estimated living expenses: On average, students will need at least €968 – €1338 per month. Also see financial matters. Please note that public transport is not free for exchange students, but discount cards are available.
All students are obliged to have health insurance to cover any medical costs incurred during their stay. We strongly recommend that students ensure they have medical insurance before their arrival. Students are also highly recommended to take out liability insurance to cover any accidental damage caused to other persons or property.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Prospective students are advised to apply for housing in a very early stage, and to pay the housing application fee (€285) as early as possible after application. An application for housing can be submitted via the application portal as soon as their application for admission has been submitted. There is no need to await a decision on their application for admission to Leiden University. Once a student has reserved an accommodation via University Housing, the housing agency DUWO further coordinates the process.

Housing through Leiden University cannot be guaranteed, we insist that students also seek housing via other routes. If a student needs to find housing independently Leiden University can give advice and tips. Please be informed that private housing can be expensive and is scarce.

一般組
本校大一以上/碩一/碩二(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
GPA 達3.50分以上。
TOEFL iBT 90 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5，A few individual courses may require a higher test score。

sections_ordered.label: 名額
sections_ordered.text:
一學期2名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 2 人)

sections_ordered.label: 學校年曆
sections_ordered.text: Academic calendar

sections_ordered.label: 註冊繳費
sections_ordered.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔。

sections_ordered.label: 注意事項
sections_ordered.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information.
Courses available to non-degree students
All courses at LUC require IELTS 7.0 or TOEFL 100 (internet-based).
Programme specific language proficiency requirements for Master programmes can be found on our website.
Bachelor students are not allowed to take master level courses.
Standard academic workload: 15-30 ECTS per semester for non-degree student

sections_ordered.label: 實用資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Language courses are not available as regular exchange program courses. The University's Academic Language Center offers Dutch language courses for a fee. Please note that the grades of the language courses cannot be included in the transcript.
Information for international exchange students
Travel plans

sections_ordered.label: 生活費與保險資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Estimated living expenses: On average, students will need at least €968 – €1338 per month. Also see financial matters. Please note that public transport is not free for exchange students, but discount cards are available.
All students are obliged to have health insurance to cover any medical costs incurred during their stay. We strongly recommend that students ensure they have medical insurance before their arrival. Students are also highly recommended to take out liability insurance to cover any accidental damage caused to other persons or property.

sections_ordered.label: 住宿資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Prospective students are advised to apply for housing in a very early stage, and to pay the housing application fee (€285) as early as possible after application. An application for housing can be submitted via the application portal as soon as their application for admission has been submitted. There is no need to await a decision on their application for admission to Leiden University. Once a student has reserved an accommodation via University Housing, the housing agency DUWO further coordinates the process.

Housing through Leiden University cannot be guaranteed, we insist that students also seek housing via other routes. If a student needs to find housing independently Leiden University can give advice and tips. Please be informed that private housing can be expensive and is scarce.

Website of incoming exchange student information
Course offers
One semester workload is approximately 30 ECTS. For exchange students we may accept a smaller workload, but usually no less than 25 ECTS.
Polish courses are available for exchange students. If they wish to learn Polish during exchange period at WUT, they are requested to officially declare it in the Learning Agreement.
Cost of Living

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Candidates will be able to apply for dormitory. They will have to submit their applications through Online Accommodation System. Detailed information will be sent directly to candidates.

The dormitory application procedure is fully arranged and run by Student's Union Accommodation Committee.

Please note that all the dormitories are located off the campus.

註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

Website of incoming exchange students
Website of study programmes and course search.
University of Warsaw does NOT offer engineering courses.
Faculty of Medicine does NOT offer courses in English.
All departments are open, but the final decision concerning admission is taken by the coordinator in each UW's unit (depending on the availability of places)
Every student can choose courses for up to 30 ECTS per semester

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Exchange students are entitled to one class of Polish language, to one class of foreign language class and one sports class free of charge in addition to courses offered by their receiving unit (within the limit of 30 ECTS/semester).
Each student is required to have a health insurance valid for the whole duration of his/her stay at the University of Warsaw. Students will be asked to show a proof of their insurance upon arrival. It is recommended to have a confirmation of the insurance in English or Polish.
Living Cost
VISA application information

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
A limited number of places available at dormitories for exchange students. The majority of places are in double rooms with a shared bathroom and kitchen.

Students apply for accommodation in the online application form. The places are assigned on a "first come first served" basis; therefore, it is important to apply as early as possible. Students who do not receive a place in a dormitory are offered assistance in finding a room or apartment to rent. For mor information, please check this website.

交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange students
Website of study programmes and course search.
University of Warsaw does NOT offer engineering courses.
Faculty of Medicine does NOT offer courses in English.
All departments are open, but the final decision concerning admission is taken by the coordinator in each UW's unit (depending on the availability of places)
Every student can choose courses for up to 30 ECTS per semester
housing_text:
A limited number of places available at dormitories for exchange students. The majority of places are in double rooms with a shared bathroom and kitchen.

Students apply for accommodation in the online application form. The places are assigned on a "first come first served" basis; therefore, it is important to apply as early as possible. Students who do not receive a place in a dormitory are offered assistance in finding a room or apartment to rent. For mor information, please check this website.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Spanish Language Courses
Living in Madrid
Living expenses in Madrid (including sharing an apartment, food and transportation) would be around 700/800 €/month.
UC3M does not provide insurance for incoming exchange students, who should ensure they have comprehensive cover for the duration of their program. The exchange student must be covered by an illness or accidents travel assistance insurance covering repatriation expenses due to illness or accident, healthcare derived from professional activities.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
We strongly recommend you secure your housing before arriving in Madrid.

1) UC3M Residence Halls: There are two residence halls in Getafe and one in Leganes. Although priority was previously given to full-year students, due to growing capacity, they are now increasingly able to accommodate semester students as well.
UC3M Residence Halls Information

2) Private Residence Halls: Not affiliated with the UC3M but still very close to the Campus and already in use by many international partners.
Getafe:
LIV Student Getafe
MiCampus Getafe
Amro Getafe
Leganes:
MiCampus Leganés
3) Housing agencies: The ones listed on our webpage are the ones the University has signed collaboration agreements with, so these are the ones we strongly recommend using, in case the students want to use their services to share an apartment in Madrid, Getafe, or Leganes, or request options other than living in a residence hall:
UC3M Housing Agencies

sections_ordered.text:
Spanish Language Courses
Living in Madrid
Living expenses in Madrid (including sharing an apartment, food and transportation) would be around 700/800 €/month.
UC3M does not provide insurance for incoming exchange students, who should ensure they have comprehensive cover for the duration of their program. The exchange student must be covered by an illness or accidents travel assistance insurance covering repatriation expenses due to illness or accident, healthcare derived from professional activities.

sections_ordered.label: 住宿資訊
sections_ordered.text:
We strongly recommend you secure your housing before arriving in Madrid.

1) UC3M Residence Halls: There are two residence halls in Getafe and one in Leganes. Although priority was previously given to full-year students, due to growing capacity, they are now increasingly able to accommodate semester students as well.
UC3M Residence Halls Information

2) Private Residence Halls: Not affiliated with the UC3M but still very close to the Campus and already in use by many international partners.
Getafe:
LIV Student Getafe
MiCampus Getafe
Amro Getafe
Leganes:
MiCampus Leganés
3) Housing agencies: The ones listed on our webpage are the ones the University has signed collaboration agreements with, so these are the ones we strongly recommend using, in case the students want to use their services to share an apartment in Madrid, Getafe, or Leganes, or request options other than living in a residence hall:

Course information
Specific Limits, as well as subject to Faculty's decision:
Medicine and Nursing (2 exchange students per year each partner University, accepted only exceptionally, and the syllabus has to be similar in both institutions and high level of Spanish is required)
Dentistry (accepted only exceptionally, and the syllabuses have to be similar in both institutions, high level of Spanish is required)
Media Studies (specific selection process at the Faculty and only 2 exchange students in total are accepted)
Journalism (specific selection process at the Faculty and only 4 exchange students in total are accepted)
Psychology (1 exchange student per year each partner University)
Studies subject to Faculty's decision:
Degree in Social Work
Psychology

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Language courses for exchange students: Spanish courses at the Modern Languages Centre have special fees for exchange students: 50€ for one course; 100€ for every extra course (fees may change for next year)
Visa is required for stays longer than 90 days. The document needed from the USC is the acceptance letter; the students will have to contact the Spanish Consulate closer to their area and apply for the visa themselves.
Campus de Santiago
Campus de Lugo

links.text: Visa is required for stays longer than 90 days

保險及生活費資訊.label: 保險及生活費資訊
保險及生活費資訊.text:
Compulsory 20€-insurance for all mobility students; to be paid either at registration in Santiago/Lugo or beforehand. Valid for visa purposes.
Cost of Living

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text: Not guaranteed, but students can apply for housing. More information here.

此校開放予第二次出國交換之同學選填.label: 此校開放予第二次出國交換之同學選填

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
西語組
本校大一以上(僅限大學部)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 79 (接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級。
或
具西班牙語檢定成績 B1(中級) 級以上。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text: 一學期0名。（因交換學生往來不平衡，本次甄選不開放申請）

學校年曆.label: 學校年曆
學校年曆.text:
Academic Calendar (> Academic calendar)
It is divided in three terms: September – December | January – March | April – June
Each term consists of 10 weeks of classes and 2 weeks for the exams.
Orientation days are planned to welcome exchange students at the beginning of each term. Attendance to these sessions is compulsory.

*請特別留意該校為三學期制，本學期甄選錄取者交換期為該校Term1

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Courses are taught in English, Spanish or Catalan.
Website of incoming exchange students information
Academic information
Usual workload: 60 ECTS per year - 20 ECTS per term.
Exchange students can enrol in Spanish and Catalan courses at the Languages UPF programme. There are specific courses targeting UPF exchange students at a reduced price.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Living in Barcelona
All international students, including Erasmus, are required to have full coverage of eventual risks during their stay in Spain and UPF will check that at least an accident and travel insurance has been contracted to complement the EHIC. More information here.
Students should plan the following monthly expenses (approximate):

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text: The Mobility and Reception Office does not provide any accommodation service directly. However, UPF is a member of the BCU, a public service that assists international students coming to Barcelona. It manages the BCU Housing Service with a wide range of flats and rooms to rent and information about student residence halls. UPF has direct agreements with some residence halls. More information check here.

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
西語組
本校大一以上/碩一(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 85 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0，僅具備英語能力者請詳閱注意事項。
或
具西班牙語檢定成績 B1(中級) 級以上，B2 required for the following studies: Master's programs / Faculty of Economics and Business Studies / Faculty of Arts and Humanities for the following degrees: Hispanic Studies, Modern Languages, Culture and Communication.。
不接受醫學院、公共衛生學院之學生申請。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期1名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 1 人)

學校年曆.label: 學校年曆
學校年曆.text: Academic Calendar

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

該校絕大多數課程為西語授課，僅具英語能力者務必自行確認有充足課程可選讀
In general terms, the limited offer of courses taught in English means that exchange students must take part of their courses in Spanish and for this, they must have at sufficient level of Spanish to follow their courses.
Website of incoming exchange student information
English-taught courses
NOT open to exchange students: Faculty of Medicine and Faculty of Teacher Training

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
The Language Service offers Spanish language courses at a reduced price for students from partner universities. More information is available on the Language Service website. Places are limited and assigned on a first-come, first-served basis.
The Study Abroad Unit offers a two-week intensive Spanish course called "Brush Up on Your Spanish" before the start of each semester, in both August and January. More information available at Brush-up on your Spanish | UAM.
When a non-EU student arrives in Spain, they must have Health Insurance that includes Healthcare coverage throughout their stay at UAM. You will find many companies with which to contract health insurance. If you do not know what insurance to hire, you can consult the OnCampus Insurance, with prices that are quite affordable for university students.
Visa information
General Information

住宿資訊.text: Students must arrange for housing on their own with Housing Office support. More information here.

註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information
All admitted students will be registered at the host university's School of Translation.(Faculty of Translation and Interpreting)
*The agreement is only open to the Faculty of Translation and Interpreting. Students must enrol at least a 50% of their credits there.
You can also consider the UAB International Tracks pAll admitted students will be registered at the host university's School of Translation.rogram if you do not intend to register at the Faculty of Translation and Interpreting 
Lectures at the UAB are mainly taught either in Catalan or in Spanish. The usage of either language depends only on the lecturer who gives the course. There are some lessons offered in English as well. For a general approach to the Bachelor's degrees courses delivered in English you can go here.
The acceptance letter does not guarantee the students can take all the courses they would like to. The Exchange Office at each faculty will contact the accepted students regarding the availability and selection of the courses.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Exchange students have the right to enrol on an Initial-level Catalan course free of charge. Additional
Catalan courses and all Spanish courses are also available and subsidised. In-person enrolment only.
Limited spaces. No reservation by phone or email is accepted.
VISA application information
International Suppport Service website

links.text: Catalan courses and all Spanish courses

保險及生活費資訊.label: 保險及生活費資訊
保險及生活費資訊.text:
Exchange students are required to purchase a compulsory insurance plan (p.8)
Cost of Living (monthly) (p.5):
Accommodation: 250-500€
Food: 250-350€
Transport: 60-80€
Personal and leisure expenses: 160-220€

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
UAB offers on-campus accommodation through the student residence hall Vila Universitària. Vila Universitària also offers off-campus accommodation options.

Barcelona Centre Universitari - Barcelona Housing Service for Students

links.text: Barcelona Centre Universitari - Barcelona Housing Service for Students

Faculty of Science and Engineering
Faculty of Arts and Sciences:
Autumn semester: 17 August 2026 – 24 January 2027
Spring semester: 25 January 2027 – 13 June 2027

Note: Arrival Day/s and Orientation Programme take place a few days before the actual start of the semester. Students studying the Intensive course in Swedish (before the regular semester starts) have separate (earlier) arrival days and Orientation Programme (compact version). This course starts at the beginning of August and at the beginning of January each academic year.

Exchange studies at LiU
Only courses found in the course catalouge and marked "available for exchange students" are open to exchange students.
Only Faculty of Arts & Sciences & Faculty of Science and Engineering are open to NTU students
Exchange students should study 30 ECTS at LiU (ca. 4-5 courses), irrespective of how many credits they need to transfer back to their degrees.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Linköping University does NOT guarantee accommodation for all their incoming exchange students. We do, however, have a limited number of rooms that we offer exchange students on a first come, first served basis. Students apply for these rooms by using the same application form as for your courses.

The rooms are located in student corridors, with shared kitchens/living room and within walking/biking distance from the University. They are furnished and have a monthly rent of approximately 4128-4955 SEK (~€365-440). A number of small studios are also available and have a monthly rent of approximately 5000 -6608 SEK (~€440-580).

If a student is not offered a room from the University, they will get instructions from the housing coordinator at Linköping University on how to search for accommodation on their own.

More information here.

注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website of incoming exchange student information.
Website of course information.
Most Bachelor programmes are NOT open for exchange students because teaching language is Swedish. Only 1 Bachelor programme Information and Communication Technology is open for exchange, most exchange students no matter which level they are studying at home university, they might have to choose courses at Master's level at KTH.
Architecture programme is NOT open for exchange student.
It is advised that students make a Learning Agreement/Study plan including only 30 credits per semester. KTH cannot guarantee student's admittance to courses exceeding this 30 credits limit. 30 credits is a full time study load and is also a requirement in order to get a Residence Permit for studies in Sweden.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Language courses for international and exchange students
Cost of living: SEK 9,450-12,350 per month
It is very important that all international students have complete insurance coverage for their entire stay in Sweden. Consequently, KTH asks all international students to have a complete insurance cover when arriving to KTH. Important areas that the insurance should cover are: personal injury, medical and dental care, accidents, personal belongings (luggage etc.), liability coverage, legal expense coverage, home transportation (in case of injury or illness). As a compliment the student is covered by the Student IN insurance provided by Kammarkollegiet.

申請資格.label: 申請資格
申請資格.text:
一般組
本校大一以上/碩一以上/博一以上學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 92 (接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5 或 全民英檢中高級 或 培力英檢B2(中高級)。
不接受社會科學院經濟學系、社會科學院經濟學研究所(其餘系所之修課限制，請詳閱注意事項說明)之學生申請。

名額.label: 名額
名額.text:
一學期1名。
(限定交換第二學期人數 1 人)

學校年曆.label: 學校年曆
學校年曆.text: Academic Calendar

註冊繳費.label: 註冊繳費
註冊繳費.text: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔

注意事項.label: 注意事項
注意事項.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website for incoming exchange student information.
Useful information about courses for exchange students is listed here: 60 credits should be selected in order to have reserve options.
Please note, the Swedish Migration Agency requires students to be accepted to full–time studies to issue residence permits for non-EU/EES students. Full-time study means 30 credits (or higher education points) per term. You cannot receive a residence permit for distance learning or online courses. 
Restricted subject areas for exchange students:
Business Studies – entry requirements vary considerably and are generally high. Master level courses require a finished Bachelor's degree majoring in business.
Biomedicine – Students must have a very strong academic background. Limited space.
Economics – no courses available from the Department of Economics.
Government – limited space on certain courses
History – limited space on certain courses.
Law – entry requirement is a minimum of two years of completed full time Law studies.
Medicine – Clinical Medicine courses are not offered on university-wide exchange agreements. No entry-level courses taught in English. Students must have a strong academic background within Medicine and meet the entry requirements described in the course description.
Natural Sciences including Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Engineering - few to no entry-level courses taught in English. Students must have a very strong academic background within these subjects and meet the entry requirements described in the course description.
Pharmacy – Students must have a very strong academic background. Limited space.
Philosophy – only few courses available with high entry requirements.
Psychology – only few courses, on a basic level, taught in English with very limited space on courses.
Sociology – only few courses available with high entry requirements.
Please note: there is NO add-and-drop period at Uppsala University.

實用資訊.label: 實用資訊
實用資訊.text:
Basic Swedish language courses run part time and as afternoon/evening classes, throughout both terms and are worth 7.5 credits. There are several levels available depending on Swedish language skills.
Housing, financing and health care
insurance: All exchange students who are physically in Sweden conducting on campus studies are automatically covered by an insurance policy taken by Uppsala University, Student IN, covering emergency medical care (for non-EU/EES citizens), liability coverage, legal coverage and accident insurance. No purchase is necessary for the student. For full details about the Student IN insurance, please visit this website.

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
Housing is not guaranteed, but accommodation is offered for exchange students based on the following conditions:
1. The exchange agreement is relatively well balanced with regard to incoming / outgoing students.
2. The application for housing in Uppsala is submitted on time and the student has accepted the offer by the deadline specified in the housing offer.

sections_ordered.label: 注意事項
sections_ordered.text:
交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應的系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。
Website for incoming exchange student information.
Useful information about courses for exchange students is listed here: 60 credits should be selected in order to have reserve options.
Please note, the Swedish Migration Agency requires students to be accepted to full–time studies to issue residence permits for non-EU/EES students. Full-time study means 30 credits (or higher education points) per term. You cannot receive a residence permit for distance learning or online courses. 
Restricted subject areas for exchange students:
Business Studies – entry requirements vary considerably and are generally high. Master level courses require a finished Bachelor's degree majoring in business.
Biomedicine – Students must have a very strong academic background. Limited space.
Economics – no courses available from the Department of Economics.
Government – limited space on certain courses
History – limited space on certain courses.
Law – entry requirement is a minimum of two years of completed full time Law studies.
Medicine – Clinical Medicine courses are not offered on university-wide exchange agreements. No entry-level courses taught in English. Students must have a strong academic background within Medicine and meet the entry requirements described in the course description.
Natural Sciences including Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Engineering - few to no entry-level courses taught in English. Students must have a very strong academic background within these subjects and meet the entry requirements described in the course description.
Pharmacy – Students must have a very strong academic background. Limited space.
Philosophy – only few courses available with high entry requirements.
Psychology – only few courses, on a basic level, taught in English with very limited space on courses.
Sociology – only few courses available with high entry requirements.
Please note: there is NO add-and-drop period at Uppsala University.

sections_ordered.label: 實用資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Basic Swedish language courses run part time and as afternoon/evening classes, throughout both terms and are worth 7.5 credits. There are several levels available depending on Swedish language skills.
Housing, financing and health care
insurance: All exchange students who are physically in Sweden conducting on campus studies are automatically covered by an insurance policy taken by Uppsala University, Student IN, covering emergency medical care (for non-EU/EES citizens), liability coverage, legal coverage and accident insurance. No purchase is necessary for the student. For full details about the Student IN insurance, please visit this website.

sections_ordered.label: 住宿資訊
sections_ordered.text:
Housing is not guaranteed, but accommodation is offered for exchange students based on the following conditions:
1. The exchange agreement is relatively well balanced with regard to incoming / outgoing students.
2. The application for housing in Uppsala is submitted on time and the student has accepted the offer by the deadline specified in the housing offer.

sections_ordered.label: 居留證注意事項
sections_ordered.text: 瑞典居留證和交換期間修讀學分數息息相關。請確實遵守姊妹校選課規定，勿隨意退選課程，以免影響居留證效力。相關資訊請參考瑞典移民局網站。

Students may ONLY apply for courses on this course catalogue. They will be rejected from any other departments, programmes, faculties or schools. Students are also required to meet the specific requirements outlined for each course in the course catalogue.
Please note that the fields of Design, Architecture and Art are competitive and therefore students might not be admitted to their first-hand choices. If they are rejected from a course, they should email us at incoming.io@umu.se to discuss the possibilities of choosing another course.

The semester requires a fixed course load of 30 credits, no more nor less. One week of full-time study equals 1.5 credits, accounting for a workload of approximately 40 hours per week. This includes time spent on lectures, readings, assignments, seminars, and independent studies.

Failure to comply with the instructions and deadline will impact result in no support from Stockholm University in finding housing.

Website of incoming exchange student information
Find exchange courses
Admission requirements for exchange courses
There is a limited availability of courses:
at the Lund University School of Economics and Management (LUSEM). Students applying through a university-wide agreement can at most be accepted to 15 credits at LUSEM per semester.
at the Bachelor's level at the Faculty of Law.
in Physical Geography
in Molecular Biology
Courses are not available in the following fields of study:
Bioinformatics
Journalism
The School of Industrial Design is not available for students applying through a university-wide agreement.
The Faculty of Fine and Performing Arts, (including the Malmö Academies of Music, Art and Theatre) mainly admits students within specific faculty/department agreements.
The Faculty of Medicine mainly admits students within specific faculty/department agreements.
Courses for exchange students 
Special Area Studies (SAS) in English: Scandinavian studies, European studies and Global issues are specially designed for exchange students.
Swedish Language Courses for exchange students(SUSA): Swedish Language Courses can be counted within or in addition to a full-time study load.
Note, not all courses in English are open to exchange students.

links.text: Swedish Language Courses for exchange students(SUSA)

Health and Insurance: All incoming students at Lund University Sweden are covered by the Swedish State's Insurance for Foreign Students.
Before you arrive

住宿資訊.label: 住宿資訊
住宿資訊.text:
We recommend that all exchange students at Lund University apply through the following main accommodation agencies:

LU Accommodation: Part of Student Services at Lund University, offering housing tips and support.
AF Bostäder: The largest provider of student accommodation in Lund.
BoPoolen: The housing service run by the Student Unions.

It is recommended to apply for accommodation through several different agencies.

Arrival and Orientation Weeks.label: Arrival and Orientation Weeks
Arrival and Orientation Weeks.text: The Orientation Weeks for international students include a variety of activities that aim to help students settle in smoothly at the University and in student life. Taking part in the Orientation Weeks is not mandatory, but something that is strongly recommended to get a good introduction to student life at Lund University. Read more about Arrival Day and Orientation Weeks.

居留證注意事項.label: 居留證注意事項

Website of incoming exchange student information
Find exchange courses
Admission requirements for exchange courses
There is a limited availability of courses:
at the Lund University School of Economics and Management (LUSEM). Students applying through a university-wide agreement can at most be accepted to 15 credits at LUSEM per semester.
at the Bachelor's level at the Faculty of Law.
in Physical Geography
in Molecular Biology
Courses are not available in the following fields of study:
Bioinformatics
Journalism
The School of Industrial Design is not available for students applying through a university-wide agreement.
The Faculty of Fine and Performing Arts, (including the Malmö Academies of Music, Art and Theatre) mainly admits students within specific faculty/department agreements.
The Faculty of Medicine mainly admits students within specific faculty/department agreements.
Courses for exchange students 
Special Area Studies (SAS) in English: Scandinavian studies, European studies and Global issues are specially designed for exchange students.
Swedish Language Courses for exchange students(SUSA): Swedish Language Courses can be counted within or in addition to a full-time study load.
Note, not all courses in English are open to exchange students.

申請資格.text:
德語組，School of Management
本校大一以上(僅限大學部)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 79 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0。
只限管理學院、社會科學院經濟學系、生物資源暨農學院農業經濟學系之學生申請。
德語組，Schools T, I, ABLR
本校大一以上/碩一以上(不含博士生)學生。（以報名交換學生甄選時，本校教務處學籍系統上之年級為準）。
TOEFL iBT 79 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0 或 全民英檢中高級。
或
具德語檢定成績 B2(中高級) 級以上。
只限理學院、工學院、生物資源暨農學院、電機資訊學院、管理學院資訊管理學系、管理學院資訊管理學研究所、重點科技研究學院之學生申請。
